data.title: Investment opportunity: Ocean Harvesting Technologies AB raising 1 MEUR for InfinityWEC wave energy converter development.
data.description: Ocean Harvesting Technologies AB has initiated a 1 million euros share issue spanning from 2026 to 2027. The funding will support the advancement and prototype trials of the InfinityWEC wave energy converter in two existing EU-supported partnership projects. The company is based in Gothenburg, Sweden, and Karlskrona.

data.title: CMS targets 15 high-cost drugs for negotiation, expands program to Part B medications. Savings estimated at $8.5 billion.
data.description: The U.S. CMS has chosen 15 high-cost prescription drugs for the third round of the Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Program. This marks the first time drugs reimbursed under Medicare Part B are included. Medications target conditions like cancer, psoriatic arthritis, and HIV-1. Negotiations with drugmakers are set for 2026, with new prices starting from January 1, 2028. Additionally, one previously negotiated drug will undergo a renegotiation under the initiative. This program now extends beyond Part D drugs to include Part B treatments. The agency estimates savings of $8.5 billion in covered prescription drug costs if prices from the second cycle were in effect for 2024, resulting in 36% lower spending across the selected drugs. President Trump's efforts to reduce drug costs for seniors and taxpayers are highlighted.

data.title: Blokees showcases various products at Spielwarenmesse 2026 toy fair in Shanghai, featuring an enhanced product lineup.
data.description: In Shanghai on January 28, 2026, Assembly Character Toys company Blokees showcased its growing product collection at Spielwarenmesse 2026 from January 27 to 31. The event, known as one of the major global toy fairs, attracted attention from players worldwide. Blokees emphasized its dedication to innovation and diversity in its product ecosystem during the exhibition.

data.title: Bitget hires former Bitpanda legal head Oliver Stauber to establish Vienna MiCA hub, aiming for Austria's MiCA approval by mid-2026. Broker-led model with high asset standards for EU users.
data.description: Bitget EU is anticipating approval from Austrian regulators under MiCA by mid-2026. The company has devised a broker-led model with stringent asset requirements tailored for users in Europe.

data.title: US Trade Rep states prompt action if Supreme Court rules against tariffs, replacing them immediately.
data.description: Jamieson Greer, the United States Trade Representative, announced that if the Supreme Court decides against the tariffs, the Trump administration will promptly initiate their replacement.

data.title: I.M.F. predicts global growth to be 3.3% in 2026, matching last year's pace. Increased investments in AI boost output.
data.description: The projected growth rate for 2026 stands at 3.3%, consistent with the previous year's rate. The surge in investment in artificial intelligence is bolstering the overall global production.

data.title: Limited tariffs imposed by Trump on overseas semiconductors, aiming to profit from Nvidia's chip sales to China while postponing higher industry taxes.
data.description: By implementing tariffs, President Trump will receive a portion of the revenue generated from Nvidia's chip sales to China. This move also delays a resolution on whether to increase taxes for the chip industry.

data.title: Potential mega IPOs by SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic in 2026 could bring significant financial gains for tech industry.
data.description: The potential public offerings of SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic have the potential to bring in significant amounts of capital for Silicon Valley and Wall Street. If these companies go public, it is estimated that they could potentially generate billions of dollars in investment and wealth for stakeholders in the tech and finance sectors. This move could have a profound impact on the financial landscape, boosting market activity and sparking interest in the technology industry.

data.title: Trump praises ‘Mr. Tariff’ for strong economy, but experts disagree. Data shows US growth despite tariffs, not because of them.
data.description: Several signs indicate that the United States is experiencing growth independent of tariffs rather than being driven by them.

data.title: Mabtech & Sai Life Sciences partner to advance immunology research in Boston lab with EYRA™ platform, offering high-sensitivity multiplex assays & phenotyping. Expands access to next-gen immune profiling for biopharma & biotech in immuno-oncology, vaccines, inflammation, & cellular therapy.
data.description: Mabtech and Sai Life Sciences have announced a strategic collaboration where Sai's Boston laboratory will serve as a co-marketed US execution and demonstration hub for the Mabtech EYRA™ platform. This partnership aims to provide advanced immunology assay services, including high-sensitivity multiplex cytokine and phenotyping workflows, using the EYRA platform installed at Sai's Boston discovery site. This collaboration will facilitate next-generation immune profiling capabilities for biopharma and biotech companies in immuno-oncology, vaccines, inflammation, and cellular therapy. Kimberlee Parent, Head of US Operations at Mabtech, expressed excitement about this initiative, stating that the installation of EYRA at Sai Life Sciences will expand access to high-quality, reproducible immune profiling for researchers. This collaboration leverages Mabtech's assay expertise and Sai's discovery and translational capabilities to create a compelling new service offering.

data.title: UMC's Q4 2025 revenue: NT$61.81B, up 4.5% from Q3; 2.4% increase compared to the previous year. Gross margin: 30.7%.
data.description: In Taipei, Taiwan, United Microelectronics Corporation (NYSE: UMC; TWSE: 2303) disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter of 2025. The company, a major global semiconductor foundry, reported a consolidated revenue of NT$61.81 billion for the quarter, marking a 4.5% rise from NT$59.13 billion in the third quarter of 2025 and a 2.4% increase from the same period last year. The consolidated gross margin for the fourth quarter of 2025 stood at 30.7%. Additionally, the net income attributable to the shareholders of the company amounted to ...

data.title: Lockheed Martin's 9th GPS III satellite (SV09) successfully launched, providing enhanced accuracy and security - offering 3x better accuracy, 8x stronger jamming resistance with M-code signals for defense use.
data.description: Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT) recently announced the successful launch of its ninth GPS III satellite (SV09), furthering connectivity and bolstering warfighter capabilities in challenging situations, showcasing US technological prowess. The satellite was launched from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station at 11:53 p.m. ET via a SpaceX Falcon 9, acquiring signal shortly after liftoff. GPS III SV09 enhances military navigation by providing heightened security, superior anti-jamming features, and precise timing in contested environments, with GPS III satellites offering up to three times improved accuracy, eight times stronger jamming resistance, and advanced M-code signals for global defense purposes.

data.title: First Mold's US mold exports surged by 30% in 2025 with over 650 units shipped, driven by demand in electronics, automotive, and medical sectors.
data.description: In Zhongshan, China, First Mold revealed a rise in its market presence on January 28, 2026. Their report highlighted that in 2025 alone, they sent more than 650 mold units to the US, marking a significant 30% surge in exports. This development underscores First Mold's growing influence and success in the industry.
